blocks.p: In compliance with the information duty set out in Article 10 of Spanish Act 34/2002, of 11 July, on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce (hereinafter, "LSSI-CE"), the following identifying details of the owner of this website are made available to users:

table: Development and operation of a personal finance management web application.

blocks.p: The Owner is a natural person acting as a sole trader. No Commercial Registry details are provided, as such registration is not mandatory for natural persons under Article 81 of the Spanish Commercial Registry Regulations.
The Owner's activity is not subject to any prior administrative authorisation regime, nor to the supervision of any professional association.

In particular, and for the purposes of Spanish Royal Decree-Law 19/2018, of 23 November, on payment services (transposing Directive (EU) 2015/2366, "PSD2"), it is expressly stated that **FinTrack is not a payment institution, a credit institution, an account information service provider (AISP), or a payment initiation service provider (PISP)**. FinTrack does not access Users' bank accounts, does not connect to their financial institutions, and does not execute payment transactions of any kind. The financial information displayed in the application derives exclusively from files and statements that **the User uploads manually and voluntarily**.
This Legal Notice governs access to, browsing of and use of the FinTrack website and web application (hereinafter, interchangeably, the "Website" or "FinTrack"), owned by the Owner identified above.

FinTrack is a personal-use tool that allows Users to import their own bank statements, classify them into categories, organise them across separate financial profiles, and view aggregated information about their income and expenses.

Access to the Website is free of charge, without prejudice to the cost of the User's own telecommunications connection, and notwithstanding that certain features may require a paid subscription, in which case the applicable financial terms will be disclosed clearly and expressly prior to contracting.
Browsing the Website confers the status of User and entails full and unreserved acceptance of each and every provision of this Legal Notice, in the version published at the time of access.
Users who do not agree with this Legal Notice must refrain from using the Website.
Use of certain FinTrack services requires prior registration of a user account and is additionally subject to the Terms and Conditions and the Privacy Policy.
Users undertake to use the Website diligently, correctly and lawfully and, in particular, undertake to refrain from:

list: Using the Website for unlawful purposes, or for purposes harmful to the rights and interests of third parties, or which may in any way damage, disable, overload or impair the Website or prevent its normal use.
Introducing or spreading computer viruses, malicious code or any other physical or logical systems capable of causing damage to the systems of the Owner, its suppliers or third parties.
Attempting to access, use or manipulate data belonging to the Owner, to third-party suppliers or to other Users.
Uploading to the Website information or data which the User does not own or for whose processing the User lacks sufficient legal grounds.
Reproducing, copying, distributing, transforming, publicly communicating or making available to third parties the contents of the Website without the Owner's authorisation.
Reverse-engineering, decompiling or disassembling the software, except to the extent expressly permitted by applicable law.
Using automated means (robots, scrapers, crawlers or others) to access the Website or extract its contents without the Owner's express written authorisation.
Impersonating another User or any third party.

blocks.p: Users are solely responsible for the truthfulness, lawfulness and accuracy of the information and files they upload to FinTrack, and for holding sufficient rights over them.

sections.heading: 5. No financial, tax or investment advice

blocks.p: **FinTrack is an informational and personal organisation tool. It does not provide, and does not purport to provide, financial, investment, tax, accounting or legal advice of any kind.**
In particular, the User acknowledges and accepts that:

list: The Owner **is not an investment firm** and is not registered as a financial adviser with the Spanish National Securities Market Commission (CNMV), the Bank of Spain or any other supervisory authority.

The information, classifications, categories, charts, aggregates, indicators and observations displayed by FinTrack are generated **automatically** from data supplied by the User and are **indicative and informational only**.

Such information **does not under any circumstances constitute a personal recommendation**, an offer, a solicitation or an invitation to contract, acquire or dispose of any financial product or service.

FinTrack's automated processes — including transaction categorisation and the detection of transfers between the User's own accounts — **may contain errors or inaccuracies**. Users must verify the information before making any decision based on it.
FinTrack **does not replace the official statements, balances or communications** issued by the User's financial institutions, which shall prevail in the event of any discrepancy.

**Any economic, financial, tax or investment decision taken by the User is the User's sole responsibility.** Users are advised to consult a duly qualified and authorised professional before making such decisions.

blocks.p: All intellectual and industrial property rights in the Website and its contents — including, without limitation, source code, design, navigation structure, texts, databases, graphics, illustrations, icons, trade marks, trade names and logos — belong to the Owner or, where applicable, to third parties who have authorised their use.

The reproduction, distribution, public communication, transformation or any other form of exploitation, in whole or in part, of such contents without the Owner's prior express written authorisation is expressly prohibited. Access to the Website grants Users no right or title over such contents.

The contents and data that the User uploads to FinTrack **are and shall remain the User's exclusive property**. The Owner processes them solely as set out in the Privacy Policy and for the purpose of providing the service.
The Website may incorporate third-party software components subject to their own licences, whose terms are respected in all cases.
If a User believes that any content on the Website infringes their intellectual or industrial property rights, they may report this to dolfinapp@gmail.com.
The Website may contain links to third-party pages. Such links are provided for information purposes only, and the Owner does not control their contents, policies or services.

Consequently, the Owner **accepts no liability** for the information, contents, products or services offered on third-party sites, nor for any damage arising from their use. The existence of a link implies no relationship, recommendation, sponsorship or supervision whatsoever.

Establishing links to the Website from third-party pages requires that the link points to the home page, that it does not reproduce the Website by framing, and that it makes no false or inaccurate statements about the Owner or the quality of its services.
The Owner provides the FinTrack service with reasonable diligence; however:

list: The Owner **does not guarantee the uninterrupted availability and continuity of the Website**, nor the absence of errors, interruptions, delays or omissions, since its operation depends on infrastructure services provided by third parties (hosting, database, authentication) and on the communications network.
The Owner **is not responsible for the accuracy, completeness or currency of the data supplied by the User**, nor for results derived from incorrect, incomplete or outdated data.
The Owner **does not guarantee the complete absence of viruses** or other harmful elements; Users are responsible for having adequate detection tools.
The Owner **is not liable for damage arising from improper use of the Website** by the User, nor from the User's breach of this Legal Notice or of the Terms and Conditions.
The Owner **is not liable for unauthorised access to a User's account** where such access is attributable to the User's own lack of diligence in safeguarding their credentials.

blocks.p: The Owner performs backups in accordance with standard industry practice, but **recommends that Users retain their own original statements**. FinTrack is not an archiving or document-retention system.

None of the foregoing exclusions limits the Owner's liability where applicable law does not permit such limitation, in particular in cases of **wilful misconduct or gross negligence**, or vis-à-vis Users who qualify as **consumers** where consumer protection legislation provides a more protective regime.

The processing of Users' personal data is governed by the **Privacy Policy**, available at alxfintrack.vercel.app/privacidad, drafted in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) and Spanish Organic Act 3/2018, of 5 December, on the Protection of Personal Data and the Guarantee of Digital Rights (LOPDGDD).
Users are advised to read it carefully before providing their data, paying particular attention to the purposes of processing and the legal bases relied upon.

The use of cookies and other technologies for storing and retrieving data on Users' devices is governed by the **Cookie Policy**, available at alxfintrack.vercel.app/cookies, in accordance with Article 22.2 LSSI-CE.

sections.heading: 11. Availability and modification of the service

blocks.p: The Owner reserves the right to modify, suspend, interrupt or discontinue access to the Website and to any of its contents or features at any time and without prior notice, as well as to alter its presentation, configuration and conditions of access, for technical, maintenance, security or service reorganisation reasons.
Where such modifications materially affect services contracted by Users who qualify as consumers, they will be notified with reasonable advance notice through the contact details provided.
The Owner reserves the right to amend this Legal Notice at any time in order to adapt it to legislative or case-law developments or to changes in the service itself.
The version in force shall in all cases be the one published on the Website, whose last-updated date appears in the heading. Users are advised to review it periodically.

If any clause of this Legal Notice is declared null, invalid or unenforceable, in whole or in part, by a competent authority or court, such declaration shall affect only the clause or the part thereof concerned, and the remainder of this Legal Notice shall remain in force, with the affected provision deemed not to have been included.
The relationship between the Owner and the User is governed by **Spanish law**.
For the resolution of any dispute arising from access to or use of the Website:

list: Where the User qualifies as a **consumer** under Spanish law, the competent courts shall be those determined by applicable law in each case, and the territorial jurisdiction legally corresponding to the User may not be altered, in accordance with Article 90.2 of Spanish Royal Legislative Decree 1/2007, of 16 November (TRLGDCU).
Where the User **does not qualify as a consumer**, the parties expressly waive any other jurisdiction and submit to the courts of the Owner's domicile.

blocks.p: Users may direct any query, suggestion or complaint relating to the Website to dolfinapp@gmail.com. The Owner will acknowledge receipt and respond as promptly as possible.
Additionally, Users who qualify as consumers may address their complaints to the competent consumer authorities of their Autonomous Community or to the Municipal Consumer Information Offices (OMIC).

In matters of personal data protection, Users may lodge a complaint with the **Spanish Data Protection Agency** (www.aepd.es), without prejudice to their right to contact the Owner first as set out in the Privacy Policy.
The controller responsible for the personal data collected through FinTrack is:
For any question regarding this Privacy Policy, or to exercise your rights, Users may contact the Owner at the email address indicated above.
FinTrack collects and processes the following categories of personal data, always data provided by the User themselves when using the application:

list: **Account data**: email address and password, managed encrypted by the authentication provider. If the User signs up with Google, their name, email address and profile picture associated with that Google account are also received.
**Profile and onboarding data**: full name, gender, date of birth, country, province, employment status and, optionally, financial goal.

**Financial data**: bank transactions manually imported by the User (date, amount, description and assigned category), accounts and their balance, the different financial profiles the User creates within their account, custom classification rules and configured budgets.
**Files**: the logo the User may voluntarily upload to identify each account.
**User communications**: the content of messages the User sends through the suggestions or feedback form.
**Technical data**: IP address, browser and device type, and a technical session cookie needed to keep the User signed in.

blocks.p: FinTrack does not collect online banking credentials (username, password or keys) from any of the User's financial institutions, and does not connect directly to their banks: all financial information comes from files the User imports manually.

sections.heading: 3. Purposes and legal basis for processing

table: Creating and managing the account, and providing the application's features (statement import, transaction classification, accounts, profiles, budgets)
Performance of the service contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR)
Verifying the User meets the minimum age to use the service
Compliance with a legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR) and protection of minors (Art. 8 GDPR)
Adapting the language, import format and suggested financial institutions to the User's country

Producing internal aggregated statistics about the demographic profile of the user base (for example, distribution by country or employment status), visible only in the internal admin panel, to better understand our users and improve the product
Legitimate interest of the Owner (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR)
Responding to queries, suggestions or issues submitted through the feedback form
Performance of the contract and legitimate interest in assisting the User (Art. 6(1)(b) and 6(1)(f) GDPR)
Ensuring the security of the application and preventing fraudulent use
Legitimate interest of the Owner (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR)
Complying with tax and accounting obligations where paid features exist
Compliance with a legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR)

blocks.p: FinTrack does not use onboarding data to automatically personalise the content or features shown to each User within the application: today that information is only used in aggregate form, as indicated in the table above. If any additional processing were introduced in the future — such as producing statistical studies or reports for third parties —, this Policy will be updated before it is activated, and the User's specific consent will be requested where required by law.

FinTrack automatically classifies imported transactions into categories through a chain of rules: first the User's own rules, then rules created by the user community (once they reach sufficient consensus), and finally a dictionary maintained by the Owner. This classification is a personal organisation feature and **does not produce legal effects or significantly affect the User** within the meaning of Article 22 GDPR: the User can review, correct or delete the category assigned to any transaction at any time.

FinTrack also automatically detects transfers between the same User's own accounts, in order to exclude them from the main dashboard indicators. This detection likewise produces no legal effects and can equally be reviewed by the User.

The Owner **does not sell or transfer Users' personal data to third parties for commercial purposes.** In order to provide the service, it is necessary to share certain data with the following processors, who act solely on the Owner's instructions:

table: Database hosting and authentication. Stores account data and all of the User's financial data.
Hosting of the application's frontend. Serves the web application to the User's browser.
Sign-in via Google account, only if the User chooses to register with Google.

blocks.p: None of these providers is authorised to use the User's data for its own purposes.
FinTrack's database is hosted in the European Union (Frankfurt, Germany region), so storage of financial and account data **does not involve an international transfer**.

Vercel and Google are companies headquartered in the United States and may process certain technical data (for example, the IP address when serving the application, or Google account data at sign-in) outside the European Economic Area. In that case, such processing is covered by the safeguards provided under the GDPR, in particular the EU-US Data Privacy Framework adequacy mechanism and/or the standard contractual clauses approved by the European Commission.
User data is retained for as long as the account remains active.

When the User requests deletion of their account from Settings, FinTrack **immediately and irreversibly deletes** all of their personal and financial data (accounts, transactions, profiles, classification rules, budgets and onboarding data). No copy is retained after deletion, except for information strictly necessary to comply with legal obligations (for example, tax obligations) where applicable.

Contributions to the community's classification rules (for example, which category is typically applied to a given merchant) are retained in aggregated, anonymous form after account deletion, as they do not allow the User who originated them to be identified.
Users may exercise the following rights over their personal data at any time:

list: **Access**: obtain confirmation as to whether their data is being processed, and which data.
**Rectification**: correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
**Erasure**: request deletion of their data (also available immediately from Settings → delete account).
**Restriction of processing**: request that the use of their data be restricted in certain cases.
**Portability**: receive their data in a structured, commonly used format.
**Objection**: object to processing based on the Owner's legitimate interest.
**Withdraw consent** given at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out before its withdrawal.

blocks.p: These rights may be exercised by writing to dolfinapp@gmail.com, indicating the right to be exercised and attaching a copy of an identity document. The Owner will respond within a maximum of one month.
If the User believes their rights have not been properly addressed, they may lodge a complaint with the **Spanish Data Protection Agency** (www.aepd.es).

FinTrack is not directed at minors under the age of 14. The Owner does not knowingly collect data from minors under that age. If the Owner becomes aware that a minor under 14 has provided personal data without the consent of their parents or legal guardians, such information will be deleted as soon as the Owner becomes aware of it.

FinTrack applies technical and organisational measures to protect User data: encryption in transit (HTTPS/TLS) and at rest, and per-profile data isolation through Row Level Security policies at the database level, so that each User can only access their own data.

FinTrack uses only technical and functional browser storage (localStorage), not HTTP cookies, to keep the User signed in and remember their usage preferences. This processing does not require prior consent, in accordance with Article 22.2 LSSI-CE. For full details, see the Cookie Policy, available at alxfintrack.vercel.app/cookies.

The Owner reserves the right to amend this Privacy Policy to adapt it to legislative or case-law developments or to changes in the service itself. The version in force shall in all cases be the one published on the Website, whose last-updated date appears in the heading.
For any question about this Privacy Policy, Users may write to dolfinapp@gmail.com.

sections.heading: 1. What cookies are, and why this page also covers localStorage

blocks.p: Cookies are small files that a website stores in the User's browser. Article 22.2 of Spanish Act 34/2002 (LSSI-CE) actually regulates something broader: **any device used to store and retrieve data** on the User's equipment, which also includes technologies such as `localStorage`.

FinTrack **does not use traditional HTTP cookies**. It exclusively uses the browser's `localStorage`, which serves exactly the same purpose and is subject to the same rules. For the User's clarity, this page describes both technologies under the usual name «Cookie Policy».

sections.heading: 2. What FinTrack stores in the browser today

blocks.p: These are, exhaustively, all the keys FinTrack stores in the User's browser at the time this policy is published:

table: Keeps the session signed in after login. Strictly necessary for the service to work. Deleted when the User signs out.
Remembers the chosen language (Spanish or English). Only stored when the User explicitly changes the language.
Remembers which financial profile (for example, Alex, Dad or Mum) is active within the account.
Remembers the date range and the visible series on the wealth evolution chart on the Home screen.

blocks.p: **FinTrack does not, today, use any third-party analytics, advertising or tracking cookie or technology.** This information is not shared with anyone outside the application itself.

sections.heading: 3. Why no consent is requested for these cookies

blocks.p: Under Article 22.2 LSSI-CE, prior User consent is not required when storage is used exclusively to:

list: Allow communication between the User's equipment and the network (for example, keeping the session signed in).
Provide a service expressly requested by the User (for example, remembering the language they themselves chose, or the financial profile they selected).

blocks.p: All keys listed in the previous section fall under one of these two cases, so FinTrack **does not display a cookie consent banner**: it is enough to inform, which is precisely the purpose of this page.

sections.heading: 4. If third-party analytics are added in the future

blocks.p: If FinTrack were to incorporate analytics tools that do require consent in the future (for example, Google Analytics, which uses third-party cookies to distinguish users), this Policy will be updated before they are activated and the User will be shown a granular prior-consent panel, with the option to accept, reject or customise each category of non-essential cookies. No non-essential cookie will be installed before the User gives their express consent.

The User can delete the contents of `localStorage` at any time from their browser settings (usually under «Privacy and security» → «Clear browsing data»), or by signing out of FinTrack, which removes the session token.

Deleting this browser data **does not delete the User's account or financial data**, which is kept on the server: signing in again will make the information available once more. To fully delete the account and all its data, the account deletion option available in Settings must be used, as described in the Privacy Policy.

The Owner reserves the right to amend this Cookie Policy to adapt it to legislative developments or to changes in the technologies used by FinTrack. The version in force shall in all cases be the one published on the Website.
For any question about this Cookie Policy, Users may write to dolfinapp@gmail.com.

These Terms and Conditions (hereinafter, the "Terms") govern access to and use of FinTrack by the User, and apply together with the Legal Notice and the Privacy Policy, available at alxfintrack.vercel.app/aviso-legal and alxfintrack.vercel.app/privacidad respectively.

By registering and creating an account, the User expressly accepts these Terms in the version in force at that time. If the User does not agree, they must refrain from registering or continuing to use the service.

FinTrack is a personal finance management web application that allows the User to import their own bank statements, classify them into categories, organise them across separate financial profiles within a single account, set budgets, and view aggregated information about their income and expenses.

FinTrack **does not connect to any financial institution or access online banking credentials**: all financial information comes from files the User imports manually. See the Legal Notice for further detail on the nature of the service.

list: Users must be **over 14 years of age** to register. If under that age, they need the consent of a parent or legal guardian, as set out in the Privacy Policy.
Users must provide truthful information and keep it up to date. FinTrack does not actively verify the identity of its Users.

A single account may contain several financial profiles (for example, to manage the finances of different family members). All profiles within an account share the same account holder and the same login credentials.
Users are responsible for keeping their password confidential and for all activity that occurs under their account.

blocks.p: FinTrack offers different plans (FREE, PRO and PREMIUM) with different features. **As of today, all plans are free of charge**: FinTrack does not yet have a payment gateway and does not charge Users any amount. When paid subscription to the PRO or PREMIUM plans is activated, these Terms will be updated to include the applicable pricing, the contracting process, and the withdrawal right applicable under consumer protection law.
The Owner reserves the right to modify the features included in each plan, notifying Users with reasonable advance notice when a change materially affects them.
When using FinTrack, the User agrees not to:

list: Use the service for unlawful purposes or purposes harmful to third parties.
Attempt to access, manipulate or interfere with the data of other Users or of the Owner.
Reverse-engineer, decompile or disassemble the software, except as expressly permitted by law.
Use automated means to extract content from the service without authorisation.
Impersonate another person or use another User's account without their consent.

blocks.p: Breach of these rules may result in suspension or cancellation of the account, in accordance with section 9.

When a User creates a transaction classification rule and it reaches sufficient consensus among different Users, FinTrack incorporates it **in anonymous, aggregated form** into the community's classification rules, showing it to other Users as an automatic category suggestion.

The User retains ownership of their contributions but grants FinTrack a **free, non-exclusive, worldwide licence** to use, store and display that contribution in anonymous, aggregated form, without the originating User ever being identified. If the User deletes their account, their personal data is deleted entirely; the aggregated, anonymous contribution may remain, as set out in the Privacy Policy.

FinTrack is a personal organisation tool and **does not provide financial, investment, tax or legal advice of any kind**. The corresponding section of the Legal Notice, which sets out this limitation in greater detail, forms an integral part of these Terms.

Intellectual and industrial property rights in FinTrack belong to the Owner, as described in the Legal Notice. Data and content that the User enters into the application remain the User's property, without prejudice to the licence described in section 6 for contributions to the community.

The Owner may suspend or cancel a User's account, with prior notice where reasonably possible, in the event of a serious breach of these Terms, fraudulent or abusive use of the service, or where required by law.

Users may cancel their account at any time from Settings → delete account. Deletion is **immediate and irreversible** and removes all of the User's personal and financial data, as described in the Privacy Policy.

sections.heading: 10. Service availability and limitation of liability

blocks.p: FinTrack is provided "as is", without guaranteeing uninterrupted availability. The Owner's limitation of liability is governed by the corresponding section of the Legal Notice, which applies in full to these Terms.

The Owner reserves the right to amend these Terms at any time, to adapt them to legislative or case-law developments or to changes in the service itself. The version in force shall in all cases be the one published on the Website, whose last-updated date appears in the heading.
These Terms are governed by **Spanish law**.

list: Where the User qualifies as a **consumer**, the competent courts shall be those determined by applicable law in each case, in accordance with Article 90.2 of Royal Legislative Decree 1/2007 (TRLGDCU).
Where the User **does not qualify as a consumer**, the parties submit to the courts of the Owner's domicile.

blocks.p: For any question about these Terms and Conditions, Users may write to dolfinapp@gmail.com.
